subject: UX & Research
title: Mobile-First Design
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Mobile-First Design.

hook.text: Luke Wroblewski coined 'Mobile First' in 2009. Design for the smallest screen first, then scale up.

buildup.text: The small screen forces prioritization. You can't fit everything, so you must choose what matters most.

discovery.text: Mobile constraints improve desktop too. A focused mobile layout scales up cleaner than a cluttered desktop shrinks.

twist.text: Mobile-first isn't just about screens. It's about context: one hand, on a bus, three seconds of attention.

climax.text: Designing for constraints first produces better products across all sizes. Limitation drives clarity.

punchline.text: Design for the smallest screen first. Constraints force clarity.

quiz.question: Why does mobile-first design improve desktop experiences too?
options: The small-screen constraints force prioritization that produces cleaner designs at all sizes
Mobile CSS is simpler to write than desktop CSS
Users always visit mobile sites before desktop sites
